rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee statements in component type annexes; use assign only in implementation annexes for output features or auxiliary variables
Use only identifiers visible in the current component scope; do not reference AADL properties as AGREE variables
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks are scoped to their owning component and must reference only visible symbols
Component type contracts should express input assumptions and output guarantees using concrete bounds or visible relations
Implementation contracts use assign for output bindings but avoid guarantee statements in implementation scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;`, `guarantee "label": expression;`, `eq name : type = expression;`, `const name : type = value;`, and `assign output = expression;`
AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within component type or implementation annexes
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms/us/Hz/Bytes) as AGREE variables
Do not copy identifiers from examples or invent new ports/components not present in the AADL model
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters not directly exposed as typed ports or constants
Model fusion and component implementation insertion locations
